primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder - CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Payer Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy was documented for only 8 weeks. Many BCBS policies for CPT 29827 require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of formal PT, but the absence of specific session counts or a structured home exercise program may prompt a reviewer to question adequacy.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the total number of PT sessions attended, the structured program content, and confirm that the 8-week course represents a full, supervised trial rather than a partial one.

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection entry does not include a duration of treatment or a follow-up interval, only a single administration date placeholder (11/05/2024). Payers may flag incomplete injection records as insufficient evidence of a structured conservative care trial.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document the follow-up interval after the 11/05/2024 injection, confirm it was a single injection attempt, and note whether a second injection was considered or contraindicated.

denial_risk_flags.label: Work Injury — Payer Coordination Required
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart documents a work-related lifting injury as the mechanism of onset. BCBS may deny the claim if workers' compensation is the primary payer, as the injury may fall under a workers' comp policy rather than the group health plan.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Clarify in the chart whether a workers' compensation claim has been filed and confirm that BCBS is the appropriate primary payer for this procedure. Include a coordination of benefits statement if applicable.

denial_risk_flags.label: Tear Size Supports Urgency — Document Retraction Risk
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The MRI documents a 2.1 cm full-thickness tear with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Payers may not automatically recognize the clinical urgency; without explicit documentation of progression risk, reviewers may not prioritize surgical approval.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart note explicitly stating that further delay risks increased retraction, fatty infiltration, or irreparability, citing the current MRI findings dated 01/22/2025 as the basis for surgical urgency.

diagnosis_codes.note: M75.121 (full-thickness rotator cuff tear, right shoulder) is a clinically appropriate and direct indication for CPT 29827 arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: 2 of 2 duration-eligible treatments have an explicit numeric duration (100%). Meets the 50% threshold.

surgical_approach.note: The surgical approach is explicitly documented as 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair,' which is a specific, procedure-appropriate technique consistent with CPT 29827.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 is a recognized orthopedic surgical code.
